items.name: Breath Awareness
items.description: Focus on the sensation of breathing — the simplest and most accessible form
items.difficulty: Easy — just focus on your breath
items.developer_benefit: Builds sustained attention, reduces context-switching impulse

items.name: Body Scan
items.description: Slowly move attention through each body part, noticing sensations
items.best_for: Physical tension, pre-sleep relaxation, body awareness
items.developer_benefit: Releases sitting-related tension (jaw, shoulders, hands, back)

items.name: Walking Meditation
items.description: Walk slowly, focusing on the sensation of each step
items.best_for: Developers who can't sit still, outdoor breaks, transition between tasks
items.developer_benefit: Breaks up sitting, combines physical movement with focus training

items.name: Loving-Kindness (Metta)
items.description: Repeat phrases of goodwill toward yourself and others
items.developer_benefit: Reduces imposter syndrome, improves empathy in code reviews

items.name: Noting Practice
items.description: Mentally label thoughts and sensations as they arise ('thinking,' 'planning,' 'tension')
items.best_for: Racing thoughts, anxiety, understanding your mental patterns
items.difficulty: Medium — requires attention and labeling skill
items.developer_benefit: Makes mental patterns visible, helps catch anxiety early

comparisons.insight: Start with Breath Awareness (simplest). Add Body Scan for physical tension. Use Walking Meditation as a coding break. Try Loving-Kindness for imposter syndrome. Noting Practice helps when your mind is especially busy.

keyInsight: You don't need to find the 'best' meditation — start with Breath Awareness (3 minutes daily). The benefits come from CONSISTENCY, not style. After 2 weeks, experiment with other styles to find what works for you.
